violations.text: An allergy statement was lacking on the menus. Per PIC, allergy statement will be added to new menus when printed. PIC temporarily placed an allergy statement where people order food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Establishment was bottling hot sauce. Owner elected to stop bottling process instead of getting HACCP plan. Hot sauce was voluntarily disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of the deli slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Deli slicer was taken out of service. Slicer to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized before next use. Proper cleaning procedures reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

The reach in fridge was observed iced over and unable to recover to an adequate ambient temperature efficiently. Per PIC, all TCS foods were recently placed in unit. ALL TCS foods were relocated to other refrigeration. Unit to be serviced/adjusted replaced. PIC stated fridge will be defrosted and monitored. Unit to no longer be used for cooling any foods. All cooling must occur in walk-in. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Ice machine must be washed and sanitized by end of day. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
The door to the toilet room next to ice machine is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Turkey was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit in the flip top prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Item was placed in the unit less than 2 hours prior to inspection and moved to refrigeration to rapidly cool. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

Flip top prep unit observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC had unit serviced a week prior to visit. Due to unit remaining in improper adjustment, unit taken out of service until it can be repaired/replaced/serviced again. New Violation. Correct By: 05/24/2023

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. Gloves and other items observed in the kitchen hand sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer taken apart and moved to warewashing area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

Tuna and other food items were observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. The prep unit was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

Deli meats and cheeses were observed not marked with the date of preparation in the single door cooler. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked (eggs). New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

The flip top prep unit in the kitchen was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Equipment must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Unit taken out of service until it can be repaired/replaced/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

The ice machine was observed with an air-break. The ice machine must have an airgap to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023
